title: Cosmos Sound Blob — mic-reactive living orb modulated by the spectrum
summary: A reactive blob/orb visualizer that reacts to the MICROPHONE live: a metaball-ish living sound-creature whose per-angle radius is modulated by the spectrum bins (low frequencies grow big lobes), pulsing outward on level with a glowing core and radiating tendrils. Gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable or denied, plus a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero dependencies.
usage_notes: RUN: open and click "enable mic" to make the orb breathe with your voice or music; if no mic is granted it falls back to a built-in beat. KEY TECHNIQUES: live AnalyserNode getByteFrequencyData / getByteTimeDomainData feeds render(spectrum, level, t); a deterministic synthSpectrum(u) (seeded sum-of-sines formants + beat kick) feeds the SAME render() for auto and fallback; per-angle radius driven by low-weighted spectrum bins, layered additive metaball aura, trailing fade. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: lobe size, low-emphasis exponent, COL tints, pulse scale, LOOP, and the formant centers f1/f2/f3 in synthSpectrum.
